title: The trial lifecycle in Uganda: only 46% of 783 registered trials reach patient access
primary_estimand: Stage-by-stage lifecycle survival from registration to intervention access for Ugandan clinical trials
certainty: high (complete registry enumeration with trial-level lifecycle staging)
body: In Uganda, what proportion of registered clinical trials complete the full lifecycle from registration to patient access? We classified all 783 interventional trials listing a Ugandan site on ClinicalTrials.gov into six lifecycle stages: registration, enrollment, completion, results reporting, publication, and intervention accessibility, the last assessed by matching interventions against the WHO Essential Medicines List. The funnel narrowed sharply: 777 trials enrolled participants, 500 reached completion, and only 360 achieved patient access, yielding a 46.0% end-to-end survival rate. Foreign-sponsored trials outperformed locally sponsored ones on average lifecycle score (4.77 versus 3.86) and full-journey completion (49.1% versus 32.9%), exposing structural dependency. Of the 500 completed trials, 140 tested interventions unavailable in Uganda, constituting an access chasm where communities bore research burden without therapeutic return. Most lifecycle failures clustered at completion, where 283 trials stalled before reaching a final status. The analysis is limited to ClinicalTrials.gov and uses keyword-based matching, which may misclassify access for some trials.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and analysis code. The author reviewed all outputs and takes full responsibility for the content.

checks.name: primary estimate in sentence 4
checks.detail: Sentence 4 reports 777 enrolled, 500 completed, 360 access, 46.0% survival.
